text:
Daniel Wiffen has revealed that his final few days at theOlympicswill be filled with partying after delivering one gold and one bronze medal for Ireland in Paris.

The 23-year-old has been one of the revelations of the 2024 Olympics swimming programme, storming to a gold medal in the 800m freestyle before settling for bronze in a blockbuster 1500m freestyle final in which Team USA’s Bobby Finke shattered the world record.

Ireland’s swimming sensation then swapped the aquatics centre for the River Seine, taking part in the men’s marathon swim, which involved six laps of a 1.6-kilometre loop, totalling 10km of action through the heart of Paris.

The marathon swim was something of a bonus event for Wiffen, who struggled to hold pace with the leading pack during the early stages of the race. However, the Irish star dug deep and eventually crossed the line in 18th place, bringing a respectable end to an exceptional Olympic campaign.

Asked how he’d be capping off his time in Paris, Wiffen said: “So it’ll probably be party time tonight, party time Saturday, closing ceremony, then party time again.”



Our community members are treated to special offers, promotions, and adverts from us and our partners. You can check out at any time. Read ourPrivacy Policy

Wiffen is seemingly in no rush to return to open-water swimming. The Leeds-born athlete was taken aback by the difficulty of the marathon swim event. "In the pool, it is easy when there are eight people in a final, and there are about 30 people in it here,” he explained.

"I was about two minutes down from the front of the pack. I was like 'Do I really want to finish?', but I needed to finish it and I'm glad to say I'm an Olympic open water swimmer." He added: "A lot of people would say it's the worst race they've ever done, in terms of the currents. So for my first one to be the hardest and I came 18th, I'm very happy."

We use your sign-up to provide content in ways you've consented to and to improve our understanding of you. This may include adverts from us and 3rd parties based on our understanding. You can unsubscribe at any time. Read ourPrivacy Policy

Wiffen used the opportunity to put his name into the hat to carry the Irish flag at the closing ceremony. "I'm Olympic champion, Olympic bronze medallist and placed 18th in the open water," he stated.

"I couldn't have asked for anything more. I'm the only double medallist [for Team Ireland], so it's kind of cool. I'll maybe put it out there that I'm hopefully carrying the flag. We don't know yet but that's what I want."

title: Daniel Wiffen has big plans for final Olympics weekend after bagging Ireland two medals | Other | Sport | Express.co.uk
author: Harry Smith
description: Daniel Wiffen was one of the standout performers for Ireland at the Olympics, and he is celebrating that appropriately.
